title: Conjecture 1.1 (Erdős / Schur-Erdős Conjecture)
context_text: Erdős offered a $100 prize for this conjecture, which asserts that the Ramsey number $r(3;m)$ grows at most exponentially in $m$. The lower bound $r(3;m) \geq \Omega(2^m)$ is already known; the conjecture is equivalent to $r(3;m) = 2^{\Theta(m)}$. This paper proves the conjecture for $m$-colorings of complete graphs whose induced neighborhood set system has bounded dual VC-dimension.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for $m$-colorings with bounded dual VC-dimension: specifically establishes $r_d(k; m) \leq 2^{cm}$ for a constant $c = c(k,d)$ (Theorem 1.3), thereby resolving the exponential growth question in this combinatorial setting.
paper_title: Bounded VC-dimension implies the Schur-Erdos conjecture

title: Problem 1.2 (Erdős)
context_text: Erdős offered a $250 prize for the exact value of this limit, a strengthening of Conjecture 1.1. The limit is known to exist because Chung showed that $r(3;m)$ is supermultiplicative. The current best lower bound on $r(3;m)$ is due to Xiaodong et al., who showed $r(3;m) \geq \Omega(3.199^m)$.
paper_contribution: Not directly addressed; the paper proves an exponential upper bound in the bounded VC-dimension setting but does not determine the value of the limiting constant.
paper_title: Bounded VC-dimension implies the Schur-Erdos conjecture

title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture
statement_text: For every hereditary property $\mathcal{P}$ which is not satisfied by all graphs, there exists a constant $\varepsilon(\mathcal{P}) > 0$ such that every graph on $n$ vertices with property $\mathcal{P}$ contains a clique or an independent set of size $n^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})}$.
context_text: Erdős and Hajnal proved the weaker bound $e^{\varepsilon(\mathcal{P})\sqrt{\log n}}$ and conjectured the polynomial improvement. In the concluding remarks the authors highlight this conjecture as a major target for extending the VC-dimension approach, and note that for the special case of bounded VC-dimension their companion work achieves a bound of $e^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$.
paper_contribution: Not proved here; mentioned in the concluding remarks as an important open problem motivating future work on graphs with bounded VC-dimension.
notes: Stated in prose in the concluding remarks, not in a labelled theorem environment.
paper_title: Bounded VC-dimension implies the Schur-Erdos conjecture

title: Conjecture 4.1
statement_text: For $d \geq 2$, there exists a constant $\varepsilon(d)$ such that every graph on $n$ vertices with VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or an independent set of size $n^{\varepsilon(d)}$.
context_text: This is the VC-dimension specialisation of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture proposed by the paper's authors. The best known bound for graphs of bounded VC-dimension is $e^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, established in a companion paper by the same authors; Conjecture 4.1 asks for the much stronger polynomial bound.
paper_title: Bounded VC-dimension implies the Schur-Erdos conjecture
